comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: passes.pass2_verify: a SEPARATE aggregate verifier (one pass over ALL findings, batched, not per-finding) emitting coarse severity ATTRIBUTES {prod/debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} + the typed BINARY sub-answer set {yes|no|insufficient} incl. the cited_reference_accurate veto; agentic (tool-using) when any finding is code-grounded, single-turn otherwise; non-frontier model (sonnet) via verifier_cfg. Contract plan_review_verification. Findings presented as claims-to-test (independence).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

Three of the four acceptance criteria are met; one is not.

---

### AC1 — Single aggregate verify call per review on a non-frontier model ✅ MET

`passes.pass2_verify` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:359–401`) makes one batched aggregate pass over ALL findings. `verifier_cfg()` (`passes.py:573–579`) substitutes `claude-sonnet-4-6` (Sonnet = non-frontier) by default. The orchestrator calls both at `orchestrator.py:638–639`.

### AC2 — Binary sub-question set + severity attributes per finding; independence rules in the prompt ✅ MET

`_pass2_model()` (`passes.py:92–120`) defines `SeverityAttrs` with `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` and `Binary` with all seven graded sub-questions plus the `cited_reference_accurate` veto — all typed `yes|no|insufficient`. The verifier prompt (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md`) enforces independence: "Each finding below is an unproven CLAIM TO TEST — its conclusion is NOT asserted" and "Be atomic: answer each sub-question on its own merits."

### AC3 — Charitable-plan-reading / entailment precision gate implemented ✅ MET

The claim-as-hypothesis framing in the verifier prompt implements the entailment precision gate: findings are presented as claims to test, and the `evidence_entails_finding` binary sub-question (`passes.py:105`) is the direct entailment check. The prompt instructs re-grounding in the plan before answering.

### AC4 — PROVING CHECK that Pass-2 DISCRIMINATES (labeled set of {true finding, planted FALSE finding}) wired into the `7284` eval suite ❌ NOT MET

Extensive search found no labeled fixture set of true/true/false findings for Pass-2 discrimination testing anywhere in the repository:
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` contains only `code-quality.eval.yaml` — no `plan-review-verifier` eval spec exists.
- No test in `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`, `tests/external/`, or `tests/interfaces/` contains planted false findings or a discrimination proving check for Pass-2.
- The parent epic's comment explicitly states "acc1 gains a discrimination proving check (planted true/false findings; Pass-2 must filter the false without rubber-stamping) wired into 7284" — confirming the requirement was added, but no corresponding implementation was found in the codebase.

The requirement states this proving check must be "wired into the `7284` eval suite as Pass-2's OWN cases, distinct from the per-criterion prompt cases." No such eval cases exist.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: REMEDIATED the Pass-2 discrimination proving-check AC: eval_specs/plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml now ships labeled {true, planted-FALSE} finding pairs (linked by 'pair') where the verifier must score the false ones with LOWER graded validity (yes/insufficient/no fraction) while upholding true findings — plus a 'no_sycophancy_on_real_defects' scorer for the inverse false-negative axis. Deterministic gating scorers (discriminates_true_from_false, no_sycophancy) + a cross-family llm-judge; gold_set for kappa alignment. Wired into the Inspect-AI eval seam (rebar prompt eval plan-review-verifier) as Pass-2's OWN cases, distinct from the per-criterion finder cases. The FP6 library-contract anti-FP rule is encoded in the verifier prompt. Offline-validated by tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py::test_verifier_has_discrimination_pairs. The implementation (passes.pass2_verify: aggregate verifier, severity attributes + typed binary sub-answers, agentic-when-grounded, non-frontier model) is in place.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: PASS**

All four acceptance criteria are met.

---

### AC1 — Single aggregate verify call per review on a non-frontier model ✅ MET

`passes.pass2_verify` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:359–401`) makes one batched aggregate pass over ALL findings with a configurable `batch_size`. `verifier_cfg()` (`passes.py:573–579`) substitutes `claude-sonnet-4-6` (Sonnet = non-frontier) unless the operator explicitly chose a model. The orchestrator wires both at `orchestrator.py:638–639`.

### AC2 — Binary sub-question set + severity attributes per finding; independence rules in the prompt ✅ MET

`_pass2_model()` (`passes.py:92–120`) defines `SeverityAttrs` with `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` and `Binary` with all seven graded sub-questions (`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`, `no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified`) plus the `cited_reference_accurate` veto — all typed `yes|no|insufficient`. The verifier prompt (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md`) enforces independence: "Each finding below is an unproven CLAIM TO TEST — its conclusion is NOT asserted" and "Be atomic: answer each sub-question on its own merits."

### AC3 — Charitable-plan-reading / entailment precision gate implemented ✅ MET

The claim-as-hypothesis framing in `plan_review_verifier.md` implements the entailment precision gate. `evidence_entails_finding` (`passes.py:105`) is the direct entailment check. The FP6 anti-FP rule is also encoded in the verifier prompt: adopted-library documented contract counts as existing mitigation, dropping speculative library-contract findings while preserving genuine library-feature-maturity gaps.

### AC4 — PROVING CHECK that Pass-2 DISCRIMINATES (labeled set of {true finding, planted FALSE finding}) wired into the 7284 eval suite ✅ MET

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml` ships labeled `{kind: "true", kind: "false"}` finding pairs (linked by `pair` field) across three discrimination scenarios (D1 testing-plan, D2 security, D3 library-contract). The spec includes:
- `discriminates_true_from_false` deterministic gating scorer
- `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` deterministic scorer for the inverse false-negative axis
- A cross-family `llm-judge` (gpt-4o) with a `verification_reasoning_quality` rubric
- A `gold_set` for kappa alignment

The eval spec is wired into the Inspect-AI eval seam (`rebar prompt eval plan-review-verifier`) and validated offline by `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py::test_verifier_has_discrimination_pairs` (`passes.py:57–68`), which asserts both `true`/`false` kinds are present, linked pairs exist, and the required scorers are present. The README confirms this is Pass-2's OWN cases, distinct from per-criterion finder cases.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Independent verification is the precision gate (Chain-of-Verification / SAFE); it filters Pass-1 false
positives without rubber-stamping.

## What
- ONE aggregate call over all findings on a NON-FRONTIER model; per finding -> severity ATTRIBUTES
  {prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility} + typed BINARY sub-answers
  {yes|no|insufficient}.
- Skepticism aimed at the FINDING via charitable plan-reading (the finding stands only if it holds under the
  plan's most reasonable reading = evidence-entailment); atomic, independent, verdict-with-citation, allow insufficient.

## Scope
- Text verification over plan + findings; code-citation findings are rare in plan review (grounding owned by
  E4/G1G2/A1 per the agent-vs-single-turn bright line).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single aggregate verify call per review on a non-frontier model.
- [ ] Binary sub-question set + severity attributes per finding; independence rules in the prompt.
- [ ] Charitable-plan-reading / entailment precision gate implemented.
- [ ] PROVING CHECK that Pass-2 DISCRIMINATES (not rubber-stamps): a labeled set of {true finding, planted FALSE finding} where Pass-2 must return no/insufficient or LOWER confidence on the false ones while leaving true findings standing (independence holds; charitable-reading filters Pass-1 FPs without suppressing real findings) — wired into the `7284` eval suite as Pass-2's OWN cases, distinct from the per-criterion prompt cases.
compiled_state.title: Pass-2 verifier (single aggregate, non-frontier)
